name: Hebe
description: Hebe, in Greek mythology, is the goddess of youth, daughter of Zeus and Hera. She served as the cupbearer for the gods and goddesses of Mount Olympus, serving ambrosia and nectar, ensuring their eternal youth and vitality. She is often depicted as a beautiful young woman, sometimes winged, pouring drinks from a pitcher. Her role extended beyond mere service; she was the embodiment of youthful bloom and the divine right of immortality. Before Ganymede took over, Hebe held the esteemed position of cupbearer, highlighting her importance within the Olympian hierarchy. She is associated with weddings and new beginnings, symbolizing the freshness and vigor of youth. Eventually, Hebe married Heracles after his apotheosis, signifying a divine union of youth and strength, and bringing an end to her service as cupbearer. She is revered for her grace, beauty, and the life-sustaining ambrosia she provided to the gods, ensuring their everlasting existence. Hebe represents the eternal cycle of life, the invigorating power of youth, and the promise of immortality through divine favor.
subtitle: Goddess of Youth and Cupbearer

keyMyths.title: Hebe as Cupbearer
keyMyths.description: Hebe served as the cupbearer to the Olympian gods, providing them with ambrosia and nectar, the source of their immortality. This role highlighted her importance and connection to the gods' eternal life.

keyMyths.title: Marriage to Heracles
keyMyths.description: After Heracles completed his labors and ascended to Olympus, he married Hebe, signifying a union of strength and youth. This marked the end of Hebe's service as cupbearer.

keyMyths.title: Resignation as Cupbearer
keyMyths.description: Hebe resigned or was replaced as cupbearer, some accounts say due to clumsiness, and Ganymede, a Trojan prince, took her place. This change is a significant point in her mythology.

sources.author: Homer
sources.description: References to Hebe's beauty and role on Olympus.
sources.text: References to Hebe's beauty and role on Olympus.

sources.author: Apollodorus
sources.description: Account of her marriage to Heracles.
sources.text: Account of her marriage to Heracles.
